fraudReport: Text overlay / redaction (object masking): Multiple opaque white rectangular fills placed over printed content (passport number area at upper-right, signature block lower-right, and portions of the MRZ). MRZ obfuscation / masking: The machine-readable zone (bottom of the data page) has multiple characters obscured by a uniform white rectangular mask that interrupts the MRZ line structure and breaks the printed character sequence.

text_overlay_detection.location: Data page — upper-right passport number area; lower-right signature/signature block; bottom MRZ region

machine_readable_zone_tampering.evidence: High-contrast uniform white fill directly overlapping MRZ text with abrupt rectangular edges; several MRZ characters fully obscured, producing discontinuities in the MRZ pattern.

fraudCheckFindings.description: make: No camera info (likely downloaded/processed); model: No camera model (likely downloaded/processed); datetime_original: No original date (likely downloaded/processed)

fraud_reason: expiry_date_tampering: The expiration year shows a localized alteration anomaly. In the 'Expiration Date' field, the third digit of the year appears noticeably darker, thicker, and less uniform than the adjacent printed numerals, consistent with manual or localized modification of the expiry year.

expiry_date_tampering.description: The expiration year shows a localized alteration anomaly. In the 'Expiration Date' field, the third digit of the year appears noticeably darker, thicker, and less uniform than the adjacent printed numerals, consistent with manual or localized modification of the expiry year.
expiry_date_tampering.location: Expiration Date field, year portion of '2031/11/19', third digit in the year
expiry_date_tampering.evidence: The suspect digit has heavier stroke density and a less regular contour than the neighboring '20' and the remaining '/11/19' characters, which appear more uniformly printed. The anomaly is confined to the year digit rather than the full date line.
